seoTitle: How to Use Dashboards in Jira Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to create and customize Jira dashboards by adding gadgets, filters, and reports for better project visibility.
intro: Monitoring several projects at once is much easier with a dashboard built around the metrics that matter to your team. This guide covers creating a Jira dashboard, adding gadgets, and configuring filters so it shows exactly what you need.

steps.heading: Open Jira dashboards
steps.body: Navigate to the Dashboards area of Jira to see your existing dashboards or start a new one.

steps.heading: Create a new dashboard
steps.body: Click to create a new dashboard, give it a name, and decide who should have access to view or edit it.

steps.heading: Add gadgets and filters
steps.body: Insert gadgets for the metrics you want to track, and configure filters so each gadget pulls the right data for your team.

steps.heading: Customize for your team
steps.body: Rearrange gadgets and adjust settings so the dashboard layout makes sense for how your team monitors work.

faq.question: How do I create a dashboard in Jira?
faq.answer: Navigate to the Dashboards area, create a new dashboard, name it, and start adding gadgets to display your project data.

faq.question: What are gadgets on a Jira dashboard?
faq.answer: Gadgets are individual widgets that display specific data, like charts, filters, or activity streams, that you add to a dashboard.

faq.question: Can I filter what data shows up on a dashboard gadget?
faq.answer: Yes, most gadgets let you configure a filter so they only display the issues or metrics relevant to that view.

faq.question: Can I share a Jira dashboard with my whole team?
faq.answer: Yes, when creating or editing a dashboard you can set sharing permissions so your team can view it too.
